segments.text: "Five days later Ananias the high priest arrived with some of the Jewish leaders, and with a lawyer called Tertullus. They presented formal charges against Paul to the governor.
"When Paul was summoned, Tertullus began making his case against him. He said,
Your Excellency Governor Felix, we have enjoyed a long period of peace under you, and as a result of your wise judgment, reforms have been enacted for the benefit of the nation.
segments.speaker: Tertullus

segments.text: All of us throughout the country are so very grateful to you for this.
segments.speaker: Tertullus

segments.text: But in order not to bore you, please be so kind as to give us your attention for a short while.
segments.speaker: Tertullus

segments.text: We discovered that this man is a real pest, [^98] stirring up rebellions among Jews all over the world, a ringleader of the Nazarene sect.
segments.speaker: Tertullus

segments.text: He tried to defile the Temple, so we arrested him.
segments.speaker: Tertullus

segments.text: By interrogating him yourself you will discover the truth of our accusations.
segments.speaker: Tertullus

segments.text: "The Jews joined in, saying that this was all true.
"The Governor motioned for Paul to respond.
Recognizing you have been a judge over this nation for many years, I gladly make my defense,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Paul began.
You can easily verify that I arrived in Jerusalem to worship just twelve days ago.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Nobody found me arguing in the Temple with anyone, or inciting people to riot in any synagogue or anywhere in the city.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Nor can they prove to you any of their accusations against me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But I will admit this to you: I serve the God of our fathers following the beliefs of the Way, which they call a heretical sect. I believe everything the law teaches and what is written in the books of the prophets.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I have the same hope in God that they do, believing that there will be a resurrection of the good and the wicked.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Consequently I try to make sure I always have a clear conscience before God and everyone.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Having been away for a few years I returned to bring some money to help the poor and to give offerings to God.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: That's what they found me doing in the Temple—completing the ceremony of purification. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But some Jews from the province of Asia were there, who should be present here before you today to bring their charges, if they have anything against me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Otherwise let these men here explain themselves what crime they found me guilty of when I stood before the council,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: except for the time when I shouted out to them, ‘I am on trial before you today because of my belief in the resurrection of the dead.’
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: "Felix who was well-informed about the Way then adjourned the trial.
When Lysias the commander comes I will make my decision regarding your case,
segments.speaker: Felix

segments.text: he said.
"He ordered the centurion to keep Paul in custody but to allow him some measure of freedom and to let Paul's friends care for him without interference.
"Some days later Felix returned with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish. He sent for Paul and listened to him speak about trusting in Christ Jesus.
"He discussed with them about living right, self-control, and the coming judgment. Felix became alarmed and told Paul,
You can go now, and I'll send for you when I get the chance.
segments.speaker: Felix became alarmed and

segments.text: "Hoping that Paul would give him a bribe, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him.
"Two years passed and Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. To stay in favor with the Jews, Felix left Paul in prison.
